hard.owner_title: Vice President Asia Pacific Japan & ME
hard.next_step: 30% confidence Jun 9th RFP has come out today- ck to reach out to project lead- and May 04: Confirmation project is frozen due to other internal priorities. KIV and get update by July Apr27 No update on date of Tender. Chasing after sourcing project manager. Apr13 RFP to be received by April 20. Pending session on Risk management with procurement Mar 27 just re confirm Tender will come early April - decision to be made by June Mar 13- Meeting Procurement COE head next week- Tender still expected by March Feb 09 - some administrative clarification requested - Tender by March - execs meetings scheduled for RT by March 18 Jan 19th Tender will be finally shared in March. Final decision expected by Q1 Jan 12th: Confirmation short listed. Tender to be received by mid Jan (partnering with EY). Vendor selected by March. Dec 08th Project on hold until Jan end. Not able to identify champion/ exec sponsor engagement and missing clarity on next steps. Nov 24th: Meeting with Business owner in Nov 26: need to clarify next steps and timeline 05th Nov: HoP confirms process is moving slowly and Zycus to be short listed. EY (supporting process) seems to push for Icertis (working with Alliance to mitigate). Risk of delay to Q4. 31st Oct: Still some clarifications to be responded. Meeting with CPO next week. Challenging for Q3 closing. 13rd Oct: Tender is now expected by mid Nov 03rd Oct: process is going slow. Add clarification on configuration responded this week. Good traction with sourcing team so far. 22nd Sep: Working with EY to get exec engagement. Tender expected in coming 2 weeks 12nd Sep: Add clarification made. Tender expected by End Sep. F2F by Oct (PM & Exec sponsor) 01st Sep: Add clarification on implementation & maintenance requested. Still pending of Tender 18th Aug: Tender invitation is expected by mid Sep 01st Aug: AI clarification responded. Chasing for a F2F meeting by beg of Sep 25th Jul: Add' clarification on pricing shared + respond to AI clarification by Aug 01 21st Jul: Add info on AI requested (tbr by Aug 01) 15th Jul: Good overview session yesterday. Customer will come back in 1 month to clarify short list 14th Jul: Financial shared on Fri11. Use cases demo on Jul14 07th Jul: Short listed. Demo by July 14 30th Jun: Demo to happen by mid-July 23rd Jun: Pending confirmation demos date. 16th Jun: RFI responses sent on Jun12. Use case demo under scheduling by beg of July. 09th Jun: RFI for CLM received. To be responded by Jun12. Flanking approach on bundling both eAuction & CLM opportunities. 19th May: Demo well received. Ask for a first $ estimation. 28th Apr: eAuction demo by May 13. NDA signature going on. 18th Apr: eAuction/ Sourcing Demo expected by May02. Process on going 10th Apr: NDA to be signed. Validate eAuction POC feasibility (paid POC). POC to be delivered in coming 2 months. 04th Apr: Meeting by Apr 10 to clarify use case around eAuction and next steps. 03rd Mar: Focus on migrating some Oracle EBS proc modules this year. In parallel, they want/must start with GenAI use case. Working with Arcblue on "BPR". Invitation to Horizon. Call back in 1 month. 17th Feb: F2F meeting on Feb 27 to align on next steps 27th Jan: Request for a F2F meeting by end of Feb 17th Dec: Qualification & Overview demo. Still early stage. Expect to meet in HK by end of Jan.
hard.ais_why:
• Per the 21 Jul 2025 rep note: 'Add info on AI requested (tbr by Aug 01).' Per the 1 Aug 2025 entry: 'AI clarification responded.' This is a verbatim buyer-side AI-clarification request as part of the formal CLM tender ‚Äî MTR's procurement team explicitly requested additional information about Zycus's AI capabilities during the evaluation cycle.
• On 4 May 2026: 'Confirmation project is frozen due to other internal priorities. KIV and get update by July.' On 19 Sep 2024: 'Update from On Wing next week (confirmation if they open a selection process).' MTR is a long-running multi-stage evaluation (~14 months captured) bundling CLM with eAuction/Sourcing. The AI-info clarification was a real, dated, buyer-originated AI question ‚Äî but the project is currently frozen.
• Confidence is Medium. MTR has only Merlin in product scope (single AI SKU bundled with iContract for CLM) and the AI signal is the formal info-request rather than a champion-led AI demand. Project is frozen as of May 2026. Score 6 (boundary DOWN per rubric) reflects genuine buyer AI-clarification ask without an active AI-led evaluation.

north_star_verdict.headline: Frozen in May, the deal is live again and escalating: MTR issued the formal CLMS tender Q114354, Zycus executed the NDA (15 Jun 2026), attended the briefing (16 Jun), cleared portal registration (30 Jun), and VP Carl Kimball took ownership on 01 Jul 2026. But the 18 Dec close is optimistic for a full public-tender motion, no economic buyer or champion is engaged, EY is steering MTR toward Icertis, and the scope has narrowed to CLM-only.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 13 Jun 2025, qualified same day; Shortlisted since 07 Jul 2025 (about 364 days in stage). Close 18 Dec 2026 is 165 days out from the 06 Jul 2026 sweep. Project froze 04 May 2026, then the formal CLMS tender Q114354 issued 09 Jun 2026; Zycus is now actively responding (NDA executed 15 Jun, briefing 16 Jun, portal registration approved 30 Jun after a 28 Jun rejection, tender clarifications answered 03 Jul). Post-award enterprise motion: evaluation and vendor selection 60-90 days, commercials and ROI 30-45 days, InfoSec and legal 30-45 days, contracting 30 days = 135-180 days minimum from award. With the submission still closing in July and no award yet, 18 Dec is feasible only on an uninterrupted, compressed path; any further internal freeze breaks it. CloseDate already pushed once (26 Jun -> 18 Dec 2026 on 30 Apr 2026).
evidence: Formal CLMS tender Q114354 issued 09 Jun 2026; Zycus registered and actively responding (last activity 03 Jul 2026, tender clarifications answered by MTR)
NDA executed 15 Jun 2026, tender briefing attended 16 Jun 2026, hkextender portal registration approved 30 Jun 2026 (after a 28 Jun rejection)
Ownership escalated to VP Carl Kimball (Vice President Asia Pacific Japan & ME) on 01 Jul 2026, replacing Guillaume Pasquet
Shortlisted stage held about 364 days (since 07 Jul 2025)
CloseDate pushed 26 Jun -> 18 Dec 2026 on 30 Apr 2026 (cumulative slip from the original Q3 2025 target)
No economic buyer engaged; Alex Gautier (Decision Maker) contacted only via Horizon-event invitations, never on a working call
No champion identified; exec-sponsor engagement failed (Next Step 08 Dec 2025)
EY advisor pushing Icertis (Next Step 05 Nov 2025); mitigation via partner Alliance still unproven
Scope narrowed from a bundled eAuction + CLM flanking approach to a CLM-only formal tender (Q114354)
Zero buyer calls discovered in the trailing 13 months; Best Case forecast is indefensible on current evidence

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case exists. MTR requested a first dollar estimation on 19 May 2025 (Next Step), signalling early budget scoping, but no ROI, savings, cycle-time, headcount, or compliance-cost figure has been developed or validated with the buyer. The Next Step history frames the initiative qualitatively: migrating some Oracle EBS procurement modules and a parallel GenAI use case (03 Mar 2025). Without a quantified value case, the Q114354 tender is scored on functional fit versus Icertis rather than financial return, a real weakness given EY's Icertis lean. Building a quantified ROI model tied to MTR's contract volume and stated GenAI mandate is the single highest-leverage differentiation move.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is engaged. Alex Gautier (General Manager - Procurement & Supply Chain) is the Salesforce Decision Maker and the most senior mapped contact, but there is no record of him joining a working call or commercial conversation; outreach to him was limited to Horizon-event invitations (25 Mar and 14 Jun 2026 emails). No budget owner or CFO-level sponsor is named in the contact roles, and the AIS note flags 'not able to identify champion / exec sponsor engagement' (08 Dec 2025). In a public tender the formal budget sits above procurement, so identifying and reaching the budget owner behind Q114354 is a ranked move. What the evidence does show is that procurement (Alex Gautier, Navjot Singh, and gatekeeper Lilian Au-Yeung) owns the process today, not that a commercial sponsor has been secured.
sources: Contact Role: Alex Gautier, Decision Maker
Task 14 Jun 2026 (Horizon invite to Alex Gautier)
No Economic Buyer contact role present

decision_criteria.narrative: The decision criteria are being formalized through the Q114354 CLMS tender rather than captured on a discovery call. The scored requirements visibly include AI capability (the buyer-originated 'Add info on AI requested' clarification of 21 Jul 2025, responded 01 Aug 2025), implementation and maintenance (add clarification requested 01 Sep 2025), configuration (03 Oct 2025), and a contract-storage-size question (Q&A PQ114354, 15 Jun 2026). A parallel third-party risk / KYC capability is also in scope (Simon Fung email, 20 Mar 2026). These are confirmed as evaluation topics from the tender Q&A trail; the full weighted criteria live inside the Q114354 document, which the deal team should extract and map Merlin against. Criteria are therefore partially known and forming, not undocumented.
sources: Task 15 Jun 2026 (Q&A PQ114354 contract storage size)
Task 20 Mar 2026 (Simon Fung, 3rd party risk / KYC)

decision_process.narrative: MTR is running a formal, advisor-supported public tender. The process moved from qualification and overview demo (17 Dec 2024), through shortlisting (07 Jul 2025) and a use-case demo (14 Jul 2025), into a ~14-month multi-stage evaluation that froze on 04 May 2026 and re-opened as the formal Q114354 CLMS tender on 09 Jun 2026. The mechanics are now explicit: NDA executed 15 Jun 2026, tender briefing 16 Jun, mandatory registration on the www.hkextender.com e-tender portal (rejected 28 Jun, approved 30 Jun), and a clarifications-to-tenderers cycle answered by MTR on 03 Jul 2026. EY supports and scores the process (05 Nov 2025). The approval chain above procurement is not yet mapped, and the buyer has not published a dated evaluation-to-award schedule, which is the key visibility gap.
sources: Task 15 Jun / 16 Jun / 28 Jun / 30 Jun / 03 Jul 2026 (tender + portal + clarifications)

paper_process.narrative: Contracting will follow MTR's public-tender framework: submissions run through the www.hkextender.com e-tender portal (registration approved 30 Jun 2026 after a 28 Jun rejection, with an upload failure worked around on 29 Jun), the mutual NDA was executed 15 Jun 2026, and a third-party risk-management / KYC requirement is in scope (20 Mar 2026, Simon Fung). Formal MSA/SOW redlining, InfoSec review, and any Oracle EBS systems-integration deep-dive will follow vendor selection. There is no Current_Contract_Expiration__c populated, so there is no renewal forcing function; the tender timeline itself is the clock. What would create visibility is MTR's published Q114354 submission deadline and evaluation-to-award milestones, which the deal team should extract from the tender document.
sources: Task 30 Jun / 29 Jun / 28 Jun 2026 (hkextender portal registration + upload)
Task 20 Mar 2026 (3rd Party Risk Management / KYC)

identify_pain.narrative: MTR's pain is articulated qualitatively but never quantified. Two threads run through the record: legacy Oracle EBS procurement modules being migrated and a stated mandate to adopt GenAI in procurement ('they want/must start with GenAI use case', 03 Mar 2025), plus a parallel Supply Chain Risk Management / KYC framework need raised by Simon Fung (20 Mar 2026: a platform to 'develop a KYC' capability). The original bundling of eAuction and CLM (09 Jun 2025) signalled a broader desire to consolidate procurement tooling, though scope has since narrowed to the relevant stakeholder pain is real enough to drive a formal tender, but no operational metric (contract cycle time, legal-review bottleneck, compliance exposure, cost leakage) and no single pain owner have been captured, which limits the value story versus Icertis.
sources: Task 20 Mar 2026 (Simon Fung, risk / KYC platform need)

champion.narrative: No champion is identified. The record explicitly states 'not able to identify champion / exec sponsor engagement' (08 Dec 2025), and attempts to secure exec engagement via EY (12 Sep, 22 Sep 2025) never confirmed. Navjot Singh (Procurement Head - Centre of Excellence, Procurement Transformation) is the strongest candidate because his transformation mandate aligns with the Merlin GenAI story, but there is no evidence of advocacy, introductions, or pushing back on EY's Icertis lean. Lilian Au-Yeung (Assistant Manager, Procurement & Contracts) is a helpful and responsive tender gatekeeper active through Jun-Jul 2026, but she administers the tender rather than influencing the decision. Converting Navjot Singh into an internal advocate for the Merlin/GenAI differentiation, before commercials, is the developing-champion move.
sources: Contact Role: Navjot Singh, Procurement Transformation
Task 15 Jun / 03 Jul 2026 (Lilian Au-Yeung active gatekeeper)

competition.narrative: Zycus is in a competitive public tender against Icertis (primary, EY-favoured) and Sirion Labs. Competitors__c lists 'iCertis; Sirion Labs', and the Next Step of 05 Nov 2025 confirms 'EY (supporting process) seems to push for Icertis (working with Alliance to mitigate)', a structural disadvantage because the advisor shapes and scores the tender. The Zycus wedge is Merlin (GenAI contract intelligence) tied to MTR's stated GenAI mandate and the scored AI-clarification requirement; Icertis is comparatively rules-based CLM. No new competitor evidence surfaced this sweep, so the ranking is carried forward unchanged. How we win: make Merlin the strategic centrepiece of the Q114354 response, quantify the AI/automation ROI, and reach the budget owner so the AI case is evaluated as strategic value rather than a feature checkbox, neutralising EY's Icertis lean.

deal_movement.summary: The deal re-activated after a May freeze and is now escalating through the formal Q114354 CLMS tender. Since the last sweep, ownership moved to VP Carl Kimball (01 Jul 2026), MTR issued and Zycus answered tender clarifications (03 Jul 2026), and portal registration cleared (30 Jun 2026). The last genuine two-way exchange is the 03 Jul 2026 clarifications-to-tenderers cycle with gatekeeper Lilian Au-Yeung. Engagement is rep/gatekeeper-driven procurement process, not power-sponsor buy-in.

competitive_position.summary: Icertis is the single strongest current threat, favoured by MTR's advisor EY, which 'seems to push for Icertis' (05 Nov 2025); Zycus is countering via partner Alliance but the outcome is unproven. Sirion Labs is named in the tender competitor field with no active buyer preference signal (low threat). The award is being decided through the formal Q114354 public tender, so no competitor is buyer-confirmed as preferred; EY's advisory influence is the risk to watch. Zycus's wedge is the Merlin GenAI story against MTR's stated AI mandate. No new competitor surfaced this sweep, so threat levels are carried forward unchanged.

commentary.win_position: Win 42 is carried by momentum 59 vs stage-expected 56; competitive strength +0.20 (weight 15). Offsetting it: scope narrowed vs prior — The 09 Jun 2025 flanking approach bundled eAuction and CLM; the live opportunity has narrowed to a standalone….
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 59 (moving forward): Next Step carries 12 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 21 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 24 is anchored on the stage win (42), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 59, and dragged by risk 21. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

blocks.title: Deal momentum
blocks.read: Lukewarm — some motion, but not strong.

blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.read: There's a live competitive threat plus execution risk — both below.

bullets.text: EY, MTR's advisor supporting and scoring the tender, is pushing Icertis; Zycus's Alliance-partner mitigation is unproven.
No economic buyer or exec sponsor engaged; the deal runs entirely through procurement and gatekeeper Lilian Au-Yeung.
Scope narrowed from a bundled eAuction + CLM flanking approach to CLM-only, a defensive cost-cutting / phased signal.
18 Dec 2026 close is optimistic for a full public-tender motion with no award yet; the date already slipped once (26 Jun -> 18 Dec).

blocks.footer: Competitive picture: Icertis is the single strongest current threat, favoured by MTR's advisor EY, which 'seems to push for Icertis' (05 Nov 2025); Zycus is countering via partner Alliance but the outcome is unproven.

blocks.title: What moves it forward
items: Submit the Merlin-led CLMS response to Q114354, quantifying AI/automation ROI. (by 13 Jul)
Book a Merlin use-case session with evaluators Chauncy Chow and Simon Fung. (by 20 Jul)
Run an Icertis counter-plan with partner Alliance for the tender response. (by 20 Jul)

evidence_coverage.salesforce_window: Activities last 120d (through 03 Jul 2026); field history trailing 365d; Next_Step history 17 Dec 2024 - 09 Jun 2026
gaps: Zero Avoma buyer calls matched across opp, account, and attendee-email discovery in the trailing 13 months; all evidence is Salesforce fields, Next Step history, and tender emails.
No economic buyer / budget owner above procurement is engaged or named.
No quantified ROI / value case captured.
MTR has not published the Q114354 evaluation-to-award schedule.
